vulnerability.root_cause: User-supplied comment is wrapped in `Markup(comment)` and rendered into the page. `Markup` marks the string as already-safe HTML, so Jinja's auto-escape is bypassed.

exploit.method: Submit a comment containing a <script> tag (or an event-handler attribute like onerror) — it lands in the DOM verbatim and executes on the preview page
exploit.effect: Arbitrary script runs in the victim's browser with access to page globals; in this challenge the flag is exposed as window.__INTERNAL_NOTES

fix.approach: Remove the Markup wrapper so the comment is auto-escaped, or sanitize through an allow-list (e.g. bleach.clean)
fix.key_change: Replace `rendered = Markup(comment)` with `rendered = comment` — Jinja's default auto-escape will then HTML-encode the output
